description: Python library for accessing FieldWorks Language Explorer (FLEx) projects via the SIL Language and Culture Model (LCM) API. Provides CRUD operations across Grammar, Lexicon, Texts & Words, Notebook, Lists, and System domains, with wrapper classes and smart collections that hide LCM casting complexity.
rules: Install with `pip install pyflexicon`; import as `flexicon`. The legacy `flexlibs2` import name is a deprecated alias.
Initialize the engine with flexicon.FLExInitialize() and tear down with flexicon.FLExCleanup().
Access all operations through the FLExProject interface (e.g. project.LexEntry, project.Senses, project.POS) rather than instantiating Operations classes directly.
Import FLEx types from SIL.LCModel; use factory and repository interfaces for object creation.
Only perform write operations (Create/Update/Delete) when the project is opened with writeEnabled=True.
Use normalize_text() from Shared.string_utils to handle the FLEx null marker ('***').
Prefer wrapper classes and smart collections (e.g. project.PhonologicalRuleOperations().GetAll()) so polymorphic LCM types work without manual ClassName checks or casting.
Same-name fields can have different LCM types across object types (e.g. Source is ITsString on ILexSense but IMultiString on ILexEtymology) - verify the field's type on the target interface.
Handle FLEx-specific exceptions: FP_ReadOnlyError, FP_NullParameterError, FP_ParameterError, FP_TransactionError.
